name: Zhuge Liang
description: Zhuge Liang, also known as Kongming, was a brilliant strategist, statesman, inventor, and scholar who lived during the late Eastern Han dynasty and Three Kingdoms period of China. He served as the chancellor and regent of the state of Shu Han during the Three Kingdoms period. Often romanticized as the epitome of intelligence and wisdom, Zhuge Liang is considered one of the most accomplished strategists in Chinese history. His profound knowledge of military tactics, politics, and diplomacy allowed him to lead the weaker Shu Han against the mightier states of Cao Wei and Eastern Wu. He is credited with numerous inventions, including the repeating crossbow (Chu Ko Nu) and the mantou (steamed bun). His strategic acumen was instrumental in the Battle of Red Cliffs, where he formed an alliance with Sun Quan of Eastern Wu to defeat Cao Cao's numerically superior forces. Zhuge Liang's unwavering loyalty and dedication to restoring the Han dynasty, even after the death of Liu Bei, cemented his place as a national hero. He is revered for his integrity, his strategic genius, and his unwavering commitment to his ideals. He is a symbol of loyalty, intelligence, and strategic brilliance, and his legacy continues to inspire generations.
subtitle: The Sleeping Dragon, Crouching Dragon

keyMyths.title: Meeting Liu Bei
keyMyths.description: Liu Bei visits Zhuge Liang thrice at his thatched hut to recruit him as his strategist. Zhuge Liang initially declines but eventually accepts, recognizing Liu Bei's virtue and ambition.

keyMyths.title: Borrowing Arrows with Straw Boats
keyMyths.description: During the Battle of Red Cliffs, Zhuge Liang tricks Cao Cao into providing him with arrows by sending straw-filled boats into Cao Cao's fleet under the cover of fog. Cao Cao's archers fire upon the boats, providing Zhuge Liang with thousands of arrows.

keyMyths.title: Empty Fort Strategy
keyMyths.description: Facing Sima Yi's superior forces, Zhuge Liang uses the "Empty Fort Strategy" by leaving the city gates open and playing the guqin on the city walls. Sima Yi, suspecting an ambush, retreats, saving Zhuge Liang's forces.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A historical novel that romanticizes the events of the Three Kingdoms period, featuring Zhuge Liang as a central character.
